author: jakubgs
content: hey hey, I noticed that Ethereum Launchpad UI allows for pulling funds into an registered but inactive 1 ETH validator

author: jakubgs
content: but it appears this is not a valid action and doing this results in a stuck consolidation request as far as I can tell

author: jakubgs
content: i made a request like this over a week ago for a 1 ETH validator and the request is stuck in ~~consensus~~ execution layer for 8+ days now, despite consolidation queue being 1-2 days this past week

author: jakubgs
content: this suggests to me that there's either a bug in Launchpad, allowing such an action despite it being invalid, or a bug in how the execution layer handles such consolidation requests

author: barnabasbusa
content:
so basically you had 

32ETH validators - active validating - validator A
1ETH validator pubkey registered - still inactive, waiting for additional funds - validator B

Then you triggered a consolidation request from validator A to validator B?

author: jakubgs
content: I can share details in a DM

author: barnabasbusa
content: cc <@808969530608451584> don't think we did this edge case before?

author: pk910
content:
that shouldn't be possible.  the consolidation is invalid and ignored if either source or target are not active:
https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/master/specs/electra/beacon-chain.md#new-process_consolidation_request

```
    if not is_active_validator(source_validator, current_epoch):
        return
    if not is_active_validator(target_validator, current_epoch):
        return
```

author: jakubgs
content: right, and they did fail, just not visible on https://beaconcha.in/, only on https://light-mainnet.beaconcha.in/

author: jakubgs
content: so this action should be greyed out in launchpad
